title: Assistant Manager, Corporate Communications
description:
Job Summary:

This role supports the Corporate Communications team in the areas of publicity, media relations, digital and social media content development. He/She will also assist in analytics reporting as well as support in crisis and issues communications.


Job Description:

  • Media relations – Draft and issue news releases, facilitate media queries and interviews, media pitching, organise media briefings and events, maintain media databases and distribution lists
  • Digital communications – Manage and enhance existing digital and social media channels, and develop new ones where appropriate
  • Content development – Develop print, visual and multimedia content (copy, videos, graphics and presentations) across various platforms
  • Corporate marketing – Manage production of corporate collaterals, corporate advertising and branding materials, as well as coordinate corporate award submissions
  • Corporate events – Plan and manage corporate activities and communication initiatives
  • Crisis and issues communications – Support the development of holding statements, Q&As and response materials
  • Reporting and analytics – Track and analyse media coverage, sentiment, communications impact and social media traction; support the development of corporate and financial reporting materials such as financial results commentaries, quarterly business updates and presentations


Job Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in communications or a related discipline
  • Minimum 3 years of relevant experience in corporate communications and public relations, with exposure to a broad range of communications functions
  • Strong writing and content development skills across media, digital, print and social platforms, with the ability to adapt style and tone (corporate, strategic, creative)
  • Strong project management and organisational skills; able to coordinate across various departments, manage vendors and liaise with external stakeholders
  • Meticulous and detail-oriented

Requirements

City Developments Limited (CDL) is committed to fostering an inclusive culture that respects the diversity of its employees and stakeholders. As a signatory of the Employers Pledge for Fair Employment with TAFEP since 2008, CDL’s recruitment process adheres to strict guidelines on non-discrimination and fairness, regardless of gender, ethnicity, religion, or age.

postedCompany.description:
City Developments Limited (CDL) is a leading global real estate company with a network spanning 143 locations in 28 countries and regions. Listed on the Singapore Exchange, the Group is one of the largest companies by market capitalisation. Its income-stable and geographically-diverse portfolio comprises residences, offices, hotels, serviced apartments, student accommodation, retail malls and integrated developments.  


With a proven track record of 60 years in real estate development, investment and management, the Group has developed over 50,000 homes and owns around 21 million square feet of gross floor area in residential, commercial and hospitality assets globally.  


Along with its London-based hotel arm, Millennium & Copthorne Hotels Limited (M&C), the Group has over 150 hotels worldwide, many in key gateway cities.
postedCompany.name: CITY DEVELOPMENTS LIMITED
